channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 2 hours.

item.title: 🏴 Russia’s windfall from the Iran war is temporary. Ukraine’s isn’t. | Breaking Defense
item.description: Kyiv, unlike Moscow, has secured strategic advantages from the war in Iran, writes Artur Kalandarov.

item.title: ⚛️ Marine Amphibious Combat Vehicles To Get Missile-Swatting Active Protection Systems | The War Zone
item.description: Active Protection Systems will give Marine ACVs a critical new layer of defense against anti-armor threats that could also help down incoming drones. The post Marine Amphibious Combat Vehicles To Get Missile-Swatting Active Protection Systems appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🏛️ Romania enters US counter-drone marketplace | Defense News
item.description: Romania will now be able to acquire counter-unmanned aerial system technology through the U.S.’s counter-drone marketplace, the Army said on Wednesday.

item.title: 📍 Giorgia Meloni’s Populist Formula Failed | Foreign Policy
item.description: The Italian prime minister hasn’t convincingly delivered the renewal she once promised.

item.title: 🌍 High-Rises Amid the Rubble in Syria | Foreign Policy
item.description: Who is Sharaa’s investment campaign really for?

item.title: 🏴 ‘Everything Depends on Our Attitudes’ | Foreign Policy
item.description: Belgian Defense Minister Theo Francken on Hegseth, Hormuz, and NATO’s future.

item.title: 🏛️ Marines to start development on Advanced Reconnaissance Vehicle Increment 2 in 2029 | Breaking Defense
item.description: The Increment 2 iteration will consist of a counter unmanned aerial system variant, a recovery variant and one for precision fires.

item.title: 🏴 Zelenskyy refuses Russian demands to surrender Ukraine’s vital fortress belt | Atlantic Council
item.description: Ukraine rejects Russian demands to withdraw from northern Donetsk province as part of a peace deal and insists the region's fortress belt is absolutely indispensable for the defense of the country as a whole, writes Marc Goedemans. The post Zelenskyy refuses Russian demands to…

item.title: 🏴 Insurgent Offensive in Mali Exposes the Deficiencies of Junta-led Security in the Sahel | Just Security
item.description: Recent militant attacks that killed Mali's defense minister challenge the proposition of the region's juntas that Russia could be a better security partner than the West. The post Insurgent Offensive in Mali Exposes the Deficiencies of Junta-led Security in the Sahel appeared…

item.title: 🏛️ From prototypes to production: US Air Force seeks nearly $1B for initial CCA procurement | Defense News
item.description: In a first, the Air Force is asking Congress to fund the purchase of Collaborative Combat Aircraft, marking the beginning of the “loyal wingman" era.

item.title: 🌍 Mythos, not the Iran war, is the most significant geopolitical warning of our time | Atlantic Council
item.description: Artificial intelligence is already accelerating life-changing breakthroughs, but it also poses generational risks. The post Mythos, not the Iran war, is the most significant geopolitical warning of our time appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: ⚛️ B-1B Seen Carrying ARRW Hypersonic Missile For The First Time | The War Zone
item.description: The B-1B will now serve to 2037, and ARRW has emerged out of purgatory in the latest Pentagon budget request. The post B-1B Seen Carrying ARRW Hypersonic Missile For The First Time appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🏴 Ukraine’s army chief shakes up troop rotations after outcry | Defense News
item.description: In some cases, the troops had resorted to drinking rain water, relatives of soldiers deployed in one frontline unit said.

item.title: 🏛️ With unmanned systems in the forefront, Marine Corps evolves how it operates | Breaking Defense
item.description: Marines could begin operational testing with drone wingmen in 2029, and in the future could conduct their own intelligence, surveillance and reconnaissance missions in lieu of contractors, officials said during the Modern Day Marine conference in Washington.

item.title: 🏴 What a Post-Orbán Hungary Means for Hungarians and Europe | War on the Rocks
item.description: Hungary has turned the page. After 16 years of rule by Viktor Orbán, opposition leader Péter Magyar has emerged as the winner by a landslide. Ryan is joined by Sándor Ésik, the lawyer and writer behind the Hungarian Muse, to understand how Orbán built his “soft-authoritarian”…

item.title: 📍 Defense Business Brief: Satellite firm’s ‘secret sauce’ | 3D-print factory in a box | Ship-lobby ad | Defense One
item.description: Click to read the full story at the source.

item.title: 🌍 US military commanders to brief Trump on military options against Iran | Defense News
item.description: Top U.S. military leaders, including CENTCOM head Adm. Brad Cooper, will brief President Trump on Thursday on potential military action against Iran.

item.title: 📍 How Colombia can reduce security threats ahead of its presidential election | Atlantic Council
item.description: Though Colombia's recent legislative elections were largely peaceful, the security environment has worsened in recent weeks amid death threats against candidates and escalating violence by armed groups. The upcoming weeks present a narrow, but critical, window to reduce risks…

item.title: 📍 Breaking with OPEC, the UAE is now a free agent. What this means for markets and regional unity | Atlantic Council
item.description: The UAE's departure from OPEC will both impact oil markets and mark a significant historic shift for the region. The post Breaking with OPEC, the UAE is now a free agent. What this means for markets and regional unity appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🏛️ CH-53K gears up for first deployment with 26th Marine Expeditionary Unit | Breaking Defense
item.description: “The Marine Corps has a very deliberate plan for not only training, but also personnel and additional preparation for that deployment,” Col. Kate Fleeger, the helicopter’s program manager, said. “So we are deliberately moving through those individual checklist items to get ready…

item.title: 🏛️ The US Army wants to manufacture meatless proteins in combat zones | Defense News
item.description: The service "is exploring how the emerging alternative-protein sector can help meet several objectives, including enhancing food supply chain resilience."

item.title: 🏴 Ukraine to allow drone sales abroad, with caveats | Breaking Defense
item.description: Zelenskyy said he will ease restrictions on Ukrainian arms manufacturers, with regulations prioritizing the requirements of national forces first before exports are allowed.

item.title: 🏴 Romania picks Rheinmetall’s Lynx combat vehicle in $4 billion acquisition | Defense News
item.description: Romanian defense leaders want the purchase of the Rheinmetall-made vehicles to be underwritten by European Union-backed loans.

item.title: 🏛️ Army requests funds to speed development, production of EW systems | Breaking Defense
item.description: The Army’s FY27 budget request allows faster fielding and continued experimentation on key electronic warfare and signals intelligence capabilities.

item.title: 📍 Pioneering Plunder: How to Stop Modern Profiteering in Public Office | Just Security
item.description: An examination of Trump-era profiteering and a bold proposal to ban presidential self-enrichment, arguing systemic reform is needed to curb corruption and rebuild trust. The post Pioneering Plunder: How to Stop Modern Profiteering in Public Office appeared first on Just…

item.title: 🌍 An Ayatollah for the Aggrieved | Foreign Policy
item.description: For Iran’s Sunni admirers, resistance remains the appeal.

item.title: 📍 The Oral Argument in Cisco | Just Security
item.description: SCOTUS oral arguments on aiding and abetting liability for US companies that facilitate atrocities abroad highlighted cross-cutting legal views amongst the Justices The post The Oral Argument in Cisco appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 🌍 Early Edition: April 30, 2026 | Just Security
item.description: Signup to receive the Early Edition in your inbox here. A curated weekday guide to major news and developments over the last 24 hours. Here’s today’s news: IRAN WAR – CEASEFIRE U.S. Central Command has prepared a plan for a “short and powerful” wave of strikes on Iran in hopes…

item.title: 🏴 Investigation: Russian shadow airlines use Algeria as base for secretive missions | Defense News
item.description: Algeria has become a key customer for Russian arms at a time when Moscow has seen a slump in its military-export business after the invasion of Ukraine.

item.title: 📍 Protecting Civilians in Good Faith: A Joint Symposium on the Updated ICRC Commentary on the Fourth Geneva Convention | Just Security
item.description: Expert authors examine key interpretive issues and selected topics in the updated Commentary on the Fourth Geneva Convention​ on protection of civilians during civil war. The post Protecting Civilians in Good Faith: A Joint Symposium on the Updated ICRC Commentary on the Fourth…
